name: Double Chocolate Brownies
recipeIngredient: 2 oz White chocolate, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Butter an 8 or 9 inch square baking pan.
2. Line the pan with parchment paper or waxed paper.
3. Combine melted chocolate with melted butter in a bowl.
4. In a separate bowl, beat eggs with sugar and vanilla extract until smooth.
5. Beat in the chocolate-butter mixture.
6. In another bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt.
7. Gradually stir the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until well combined.
8. Stir in chopped white chocolate and pecans.
9. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly.
10. Bake for 30 minutes. The brownies may appear underbaked but will set when cooled.
11. Let the brownies cool completely, pressing down on any domed areas.
12. Drizzle melted white and dark chocolate in a zigzag pattern over the uncut brownies.
13. Allow to rest at room temperature for 30 minutes before cutting into squares.
14. Chill before serving.
